title: Exercises: The Search Problem
description: Practice with oracle construction and initial states

exercises.title: Build Phase Oracle
exercises.description: Implement a phase oracle that marks a specific item with a -1 phase.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def phase_oracle(marked_item, n_qubits):
    """
    Create phase oracle matrix.
    O|x⟩ = -|x⟩ if x == marked_item, else |x⟩
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
O = phase_oracle(3, 2)  # Mark |3⟩ = |11⟩ in 2-qubit system
print("Oracle matrix:")
print(O)

# Verify
ket_3 = np.array([0, 0, 0, 1])
result = O @ ket_3
print(f"\nO|3⟩ = {result}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def phase_oracle(marked_item, n_qubits):
    """
    Create phase oracle matrix.
    """
    N = 2**n_qubits
    oracle = np.eye(N)
    oracle[marked_item, marked_item] = -1
    return oracle

# Test
O = phase_oracle(3, 2)
print("Oracle matrix:")
print(O)

ket_3 = np.array([0, 0, 0, 1])
result = O @ ket_3
print(f"\nO|3⟩ = {result}  (flipped phase!)")

    # Your code here

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def uniform_superposition(n_qubits):
    """Create |s⟩ = (1/√N) Σ|x⟩"""
    N = 2**n_qubits
    return np.ones(N) / np.sqrt(N)

def verify_superposition(state):
    """Verify state is normalized and uniform."""
    norm = np.sum(np.abs(state)**2)
    is_uniform = np.allclose(state, state[0])
    print(f"Normalized: {np.isclose(norm, 1.0)}")
    print(f"Uniform: {is_uniform}")
    print(f"Each probability: {np.abs(state[0])**2:.4f}")

s = uniform_superposition(3)
print(f"State: {np.round(s, 4)}")
verify_superposition(s)
